summaries.91492: Root cause identified by a Flutter team member: the test fakes text input via `tester.testTextInput` but uses real semantics, so when semantics tries to update the engine-side text field it finds nothing; in profile/release modes assertions are skipped so the code crashes rather than failing cleanly. Confirmed affected on web (Chrome/profile mode). Two proposed fixes: disable semantics in the test or don't fake text input; the framework could also produce a better error or fake text input in semantics when the test fakes it. Issue kept open to track the exception log for diagnosis; related to #89749.
summaries.167904: Comments confirm the assertion fires specifically for `CompositedTransformFollower` (not `Flow`), and that the assertion check is based on render object type rather than matrix comparison, making it an over-broad guard. The author demonstrates a compositing-phase correction technique (custom layer with C * L^(-1) transform) as a third-party workaround, and proposes: (1) change the assertion to compare layout-phase vs. end-of-frame matrices, and (2) provide a way to suppress it. The `LongCatIsLooong` engine author confirms `Flow` is a gap they missed and that the `CompositedTransformFollower`-in-path problem has no clean engine-level solution. Currently open with no PR; the reporter offers to submit a small PR if the team agrees on the approach.
summaries.48464: Comments identify the regression cause: a change that enabled focus restoration upon popping a route, which did not exist in older SDK versions. The issue is confirmed on Android and also reproduced on Linux stable (3.19.x). Multiple workarounds shared: calling `FocusManager.instance.primaryFocus?.unfocus()` before pushing, using a `NavigatorObserver.didPush` to unfocus, hiding the keyboard via `SystemChannels.textInput.invokeMethod('TextInput.hide')` in `didPop`, or using `onTapOutside` to unfocus. No fix has landed; the issue remains open and stalled, with many users requesting resolution.
summaries.45556: Comments confirm the off-center cursor pointer is reproducible on multiple Android devices and emulator versions (stable through 3.7/3.3). No root cause or proposed fix is provided in the discussion; comments are largely version confirmations and a final triage ping to @Renzo-Olivares.
summaries.100319: Root cause is a race condition on Android: when a user types while holding a text selection handle, the framework sends an out-of-bounds selection range to the engine's `TextInputChannel.TextEditState` constructor, throwing `IndexOutOfBoundsException`. iOS is also affected with an `!_isDraggingEndHandle` assertion failure in `SelectionOverlay._handleEndHandleDragStart`. The crash is confirmed present in Flutter 3.10.7, 3.22.0, and is hitting production users with ~1.7k occurrences per 30 days reported. No fix or PR is mentioned; issue is assigned to `team-android` and stalled.
summaries.133930: Root cause identified: the user's `TextPainter` does not use the same `TextStyle` as the `TextField` (which applies material theme styles), causing layout width mismatches. A team member (@dnfield) initially proposed using `controller.buildTextSpan(context: context, withComposing: false)` as a fix but later confirmed it does not reliably carry the correct styles. The confirmed workaround is to manually replicate the exact `TextStyle` the `TextField` applies (e.g., resolving from the material theme). @LongCatIsLooong mentioned a planned framework-level API exposing immutable text layout metrics via a callback (e.g., `onTextLayoutChanged`) but noted a `TextEditingController` cannot be the right place since one controller can serve multiple fields with different styles. Currently stalled with no PR.
summaries.124966: Comments confirm the issue on macOS stable (3.7.x) and master: `updateEditingValue` is not called during Pinyin/Chinese IME composition on macOS, but works for English. The macOS console shows `_TIPropertyValueIsValid called with 5 on nil context!` and related errors during Chinese input. A triager linked the possibly related issue #107462. One commenter reports the bug additionally causes unexpected UI refreshes and text field content being wiped. Currently labeled `team-macos` with no fix or PR; stalled.
summaries.129324: Two distinct sub-issues identified: (1) `Navigator` placed after `TextField` in the widget tree prevents the engine from ever receiving a semantic tree update containing the text field, so no DOM input element is created on web; a fix PR #138446 was merged for this but was later reverted in #142141. (2) A separate mobile-web issue involving `InputDecoration` with `errorText`/`suffixIcon` and `autofocus: true` causing typing to be impossible on mobile browsers even with semantics enabled; tracked separately in #141975. The engine PR flutter/engine#55152 was suggested as a possible partial fix but a user confirmed reproduction on version 3.41. Issue was autoclosed by bot and reopened; currently still broken with no complete fix.
summaries.92782: Comments confirm the double-callback is specific to Android 12 (API 31) and reproduces on both stable 2.5.3 and master. The logs show `Composing region changed by the framework. Restarting the input method.` followed by duplicate `onChanged` values, linking the cause to the input method restart triggered when the composing region changes. No fix or PR is mentioned; issue is labeled `team-android` and stalled.
summaries.57609: Comments confirm the feature has never landed despite being discussed for years. Partial engine work landed (flutter/engine#43972, #44457, #44586) for dismiss-on-scroll and flick gestures, but the fully synchronized interactive dismissal matching native iOS behavior is still missing due to the keyboard running as a separate process since iOS 17/18, making screenshot-based approaches no longer viable. A third-party plugin `cupertino_interactive_keyboard` is recommended as the best available workaround. The issue is assigned to @Matt2D with no recent updates; currently stalled.
summaries.166683: Comments confirm the external keyboard stops responding with Full Keyboard Access (FKA) enabled on iOS physical devices (iPhone 12 Pro Max / iOS 18.4, iOS 17 simulator) but is not reproducible on iOS 18 simulators or some physical devices, leading to a `device-specific` label. Additional sub-issues identified in comments: (1) `onFocusChange` and `Semantics.onDidGainAccessibilityFocus` are never called when tabbing with FKA (only triggered on Space-key press), meaning Flutter cannot track which widget has FKA focus; (2) sliders are not focusable/adjustable via FKA. A commenter notes the issue does not reproduce on iOS >= 18 physical devices (90%+ of fleet). Assignee removed after no status updates; currently stalled.
summaries.150551: Root cause identified: every call to `build` creates a new closure for `contextMenuBuilder`; `EditableText` detects the builder changed and disposes the selection overlay, which removes and re-adds the overlay entry and restarts the `FadeTransition` animation, causing the visible flicker. The workaround is to use an instance method reference (from a `StatefulWidget`) rather than an inline function, or pass the builder reference through the widget tree to avoid recreation. A deeper analysis points to three code locations as the root: `ContextMenuController`'s unconditional remove, `TextSelectionOverlay.show` always being called, and `EditableText` aggressively disposing the overlay on builder change. Refactoring to use `OverlayPortal` is suggested as the real fix (#107732). Currently open with a workaround available; no PR.
summaries.138139: Comments confirm the issue is iOS-specific: when the device locale is Russian (or any non-English locale) but the keyboard is switched to English, Flutter's spell checker uses the device locale (Russian) rather than the active keyboard language, so correct English words are flagged. On Android, spell checking follows the keyboard language as expected; on iOS it does not. A native iOS Notes app comparison confirms this is a Flutter regression from correct behavior. @camsim99 suggests investigating `TextServicesManager`/`SpellCheckerInfo` on Android for the correct approach; no fix or PR has been filed. Currently stalled and assigned to both `team-ios` and `team-android`.
